review_summary: The transcript and unit summary provide enough detail to generate 2 grounded questions from the Q&A about online DPO and reward hacking without needing a separate video clip.

question_bank.question: What is described as the main technical challenge for online DPO, even if you already have a good reward model?
choices: Matching the prompt distribution seen by the reward model to the policy's actual rollout distribution
Converting scalar rewards into pairwise preferences
Making DPO use more than one GPU
Preventing the model from learning any preferences at all
question_bank.explanation: The concern is distribution shift. A reward model may work on the prompts it was trained around, but fail when the policy starts producing answers on a different prompt or completion distribution.

question_bank.question: Why does reward hacking remain a fundamental problem for reward models?
choices: Because a powerful optimizer can exploit flaws in an incomplete reward specification
Because pairwise data are mathematically inconsistent
Because reward models cannot output scalar values
Because human feedback is unnecessary once pretraining is finished
question_bank.explanation: Reward hacking happens when the learned objective is only an imperfect proxy for what people really want. Then optimization can find loopholes that maximize the proxy while producing bad behavior.
